title: User Invite Webhook Integration with Zapier
description:
Send a DTSignal event to a Zapier webhook when a user invite is triggered. This extends the existing webhook integration pattern used for login and object-selection events.

### Event Payload

The invite event uses the `DTS.salesforceapp` namespace and includes an additional `accountid` field not present in the other webhook payloads:

- `signal_namespace`: `DTS.salesforceapp`
- `userid`: authenticated user ID
- `orgid`: organization ID
- `email`: inviter's email
- `eventid`: assigned event ID
- `eventdate`: ISO 8601 timestamp
- `eventname`: "DataTool User Invite"
- `message`: "DataTool User invite"
- `accountid`: the account ID for the invited user

Endpoint: Zapier webhook catch URL.
acceptance_criteria: User invite action fires a DTSignal event to the Zapier webhook
Payload includes the accountid field in addition to standard DTSignal fields
Webhook returns a success response with a request ID
Existing webhook integrations (login, object selection) are not broken by the accountid addition
middleware_changes: Invite handler now constructs a DTSignal payload with the additional accountid field and sends it to the configured Zapier endpoint.
api_changes: Outbound POST to Zapier webhook (hooks.zapier.com) with DTSignal payload containing accountid on user invite.
implementation_notes: The accountid field is unique to the invite webhook and is not present in login or object-selection events. If a shared class handles all webhooks, adding accountid to other payloads is safe and won't break anything.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the webhook and confirmed it was working. Testing confirmed a successful response with a request ID. Integration was verified end-to-end.
